title: Create a house-standard publishable library
description: Scaffold a publishable @bespunky library — an Angular (ng-packagr/FESM) lib by default, or a plain-TS (@nx/js, bundler tsc) lib with --nonAngular — that builds to dist/<directory>, tests on Vitest, links via workspaces (workspace:*) + project references, and is independently releasable via `nx release` with a git-tag version baseline.

name.description: The name of the library. Also seeds the default importPath (@bespunky/<name>) and directory (packages/<name>).
name.x-prompt: What name would you like to use for the library?

importPath.description: The npm import path used to consume the library, like `@bespunky/my-lib`. Must be a valid npm package name. Defaults to `@bespunky/<name>`.

directory.description: Workspace-relative directory where the library is placed. Honors the house layout (libsDir: packages). Defaults to `packages/<name>`.

prefix.description: The prefix to apply to generated component selectors. Ignored for --nonAngular libraries.

style.description: The file extension or preprocessor to use for component styles. Use `none` for a non-component library. Ignored for --nonAngular libraries.

nonAngular.description: Generate a plain-TypeScript library (delegates to @nx/js with bundler `tsc`; no ng-package.json, no Angular-specific config) instead of an Angular library. Used for the agnostic leaves (typescript-utils, rxjs).

tags.description: Comma-separated tags applied to the library (used for module-boundary linting).

workspaceDeps.description: Sibling @bespunky package names to add to this library's own package.json as `"@bespunky/<dep>": "workspace:*"` dependencies (the workspaces + project-references linking model). Provide the short name (e.g. `rxjs`), not the scoped import path.
